---
title: "making graphs on 4bp End-motifs"
author: "Trish"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
#summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
#plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
```{r essential libraries}

library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
library(BiocManager)
library(plotly)

```
```{r reading in files to form dataframe for 4bp end-motifsset}
EM4_files <- list.files("C:/Users/pquin/OneDrive - National University of Ireland, Galway/Research Project/FinaleDB/endmotifs/EM_4bp", pattern = "endmotifs.tsv$", full.names = TRUE)

EM4 <- EM4_files %>%
  set_names(basename(EM4_files)) %>%
  map_dfr(
    ~ read.delim(.x, header = FALSE), 
    .id = "file"
  ) %>%
  rename(
    end_motif = V1, 
    frequency = V2
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    treatment = if_else(
      str_detect(file, "^Ascites"),
      "cancer",
      "control"),
    file = sub("_.*", "", file)
  )
     
    
  
```



```{r forming a matrix and making a heatmap}


EM4Heat_mat <- EM4 %>%
  select(file, end_motif, frequency) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = file,
              values_from = frequency) %>%
  column_to_rownames("end_motif") %>%
  as.matrix()


  

pheatmap(EM4Heat_mat, 
         show_rownames = FALSE,
         fontsize_col = 10, 
         cutree_rows = 3,
         main = "4 bp end-motif frequencies",
         filename = "EM4Heat_map.png"
)



```


```{r taking off top cluster of heatmap and applying row lables}

hc <- hclust(dist(EM4Heat_mat))
clusters <- cutree(hc, k = 3)

cluster1_matrix <- EM4Heat_mat[clusters == 1,]

pheatmap(cluster1_matrix,
         main = "Top 4bp End-Motif Frequencies",
         filename = "Top_4bpEM_labels.png")




```



```{r 4bp EM heatmaps with zscores for rows and then columns}

pheatmap(EM4Heat_mat,
         scale = "row",
         show_rownames = FALSE,
         main = "zscores for 4bp EM (by row)",
         filename  = "4bp_zscores_EM.png",
         cutree_rows = 7
         
         )

#zscores for columnns



pheatmap(EM4Heat_mat,
         scale = "column",
         show_rownames = FALSE,
         labels_row = row_labels,
         cutree_rows = 2,
         main = "zscores for 4bp EM (by column)",
         filename  = "4bp_zscores_EM_withinSample.png"
         
         )

#want to get labels for the top cluster in above heatmap


pheatmap(EM4Heat_mat,
         scale = "column",
         show_rownames = TRUE,
         fontsize_row = 2,
         cutree_rows = 2,
         main = "zscores for 4bp EM (by column)",
         filename  = "4bp_zscores_EM_withinSample_labelled.png"
         
         )
topCluster_EM4 <- rownames(EM4Heat_mat)[clusters ==1]
topCluster_EM4



  
  



```


```{r forming big dataframe to build comparison box plots for each end-motif}


#all_samples_df <- EM4_files %>%
 # set_names() %>%
  #map(get()) %>%
  #bind_rows(.id = "EM_source")

#calculating end-motif means
EM4_end_motif_means <- EM4%>%
  group_by(end_motif, treatment)%>%
  summarise(mean = mean(frequency))%>%
  ungroup()


#wanting to find the largest differences between cancer and normal for each end motif
#using the mean figure for each end motif
Diff_df <- EM4_end_motif_means %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = treatment, values_from = mean)%>%
  mutate(abs_diff = abs(cancer - control)) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs_diff))
  


#writing out biggest_motif_diff to a table (all data at this stage)
  write.table(
    Diff_df,
    file = "Mean values of each end-motif for each treatment",
    sep = "\t", 
    row.names = FALSE,
    quote = FALSE
  )
  
#pull out the top 10 differences in biggest motif differences and allow resulting graph to be in order of descending differences
  
top_diff <- Diff_df %>%
    slice_head(n=10) %>%
  mutate(end_motif = factor(end_motif, levels = unique(end_motif)))
  



```
```{r lollipop graph}
lollipop <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = top_diff, aes(x = end_motif, y = cancer, colour = "Mean Cancer Values"), size = 3) +
 geom_point(data = top_diff, aes(x = end_motif, y = control, colour = "Mean Control Values"), size = 3) +
  geom_segment(data = top_diff, aes(x = end_motif, xend = end_motif, y = cancer, yend = control), colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  scale_colour_manual(
    name = "Treatment",
    values = c("Mean Cancer Values"= "magenta",
               "Mean Control Values" = "blue")
               
  ) +
  labs(y = "Relative Frequency",
       x = "End Motif",
       title = "End Motifs showing the biggest differences between \ncontrol and cancer conditions") +
       #subtitle = "(Relative Frequency)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

lollipop

ggsave("4bpEM.lollipop.pdf", plot = lollipop, width = 7, height = 5)

```
```{r boxplots of selected end-motifs}
#top 10 end-motifs ranked in terms of differences between control and cancer
#also those end-motifs that were in top cluster of pheatmap

hmap_em <- c("AAAA", "CAAA", "ATTT", "GTTT", "GGAA")
  
top_diff <- Diff_df %>%
    slice_head(n=10)%>%
  pull(end_motif)

total_bplot_em <- c(top_diff, hmap_em)
total_bplot_em

selected_samples <- EM4[EM4$end_motif %in% total_bplot_em,]

ggplot(selected_samples,
aes(x = end_motif,
y = frequency,
fill = treatment)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Treatment",
    values = c("cancer"= "magenta",
               "control" = "blue")) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.3) + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8)) +
  labs(y = "Relative Frequency",
       x = "End Motif",
       title = "Selection of End-Motifs",
       subtitle = "Those motifs with biggest difference in cancer and control states, and those in top cluster of heatmap")

ggsave("selected_samples4bp.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)

```
```{r}

  
ggplot(data = selected_samples, aes(x = end_motif, 
                                   y = frequency, 
                                   fill = treatment)) +
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "End Motif",
       y = "Relative Frequency",
       title = "Mean Values for Cancer and Control Samples" 
        
  )




```
```{r finding and plotting top 5 changes for cancer and control}
#calculating top 5 and bottom 5 differences for control - cancer
topChanges_df <- EM4_end_motif_means %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = treatment, values_from = mean)%>%
  mutate(change = (control - cancer)) %>%
  arrange(desc(change))%>%
  mutate(end_motif = factor(end_motif, levels = unique(end_motif)))


top5changes <- bind_rows(
  slice_head(topChanges_df, n=5),
  slice_tail(topChanges_df, n =5)
)%>%
  pull(end_motif)

top5Changes_df <- EM4 %>%
  filter(end_motif %in% top5changes) %>%
  mutate(end_motif = factor(end_motif, levels = top5changes), treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("control", "cancer")))
  

top_10_cancer_drops <- topChanges_df %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  pull(end_motif) 


#plotting instructions

ggplot(data = top5Changes_df, aes(x = end_motif, 
                                   y = frequency, 
                                   fill = treatment)) +
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12, face = "bold")) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(control = "blue", cancer = "pink")) +
  labs(x = "End Motif",
       y = "Frequency",
       title = "Top 5 Decreases and Increases \nfor end-motifs in cancer",
       fill = "Treatment"
        
  )

ggsave("top5changes.png")

```

```{r replicating analysis done by Serpas et al (2019)}

#serpas et al arranged end motifs in order of descending control values, leaving out those that had no significant difference between control and cancer treatments


#calculating end-motif means
EM4_end_motif_means <- EM4%>%
  group_by(end_motif, treatment)%>%
  summarise(mean = mean(frequency))%>%
  ungroup()

#calculating which end-motifs have a significant difference between cancer and control (in order to remove them)

EM4signif_analysis_df <- EM4_end_motif_means %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = treatment, values_from = mean)%>%
  mutate(abs_diff = abs(cancer - control),
         log2FX = log2(cancer/control)) %>%
  arrange(desc(control))

#calculating pvalues
EM4motif_tests <- EM4 %>%
  group_by(end_motif)%>%
  summarise(
    p.value = wilcox.test(frequency~treatment)$p.value )%>%
    ungroup()

#correction for multiple testing
EM4motif_tests <- EM4motif_tests %>%
  mutate(FDR = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH"))

#making a big table

final_EM4motif_results <- EM4signif_analysis_df %>%
  left_join(EM4motif_tests, by="end_motif") %>%
  arrange(desc(control)) %>%
  filter(FDR < 0.05) %>%
  slice(1:25) %>%
  mutate(end_motif = factor(end_motif, levels = unique(end_motif)), direction = if_else(log2FX > 0, "cancer-enriched", "control-enriched")) 

#pulling end-motifs of interest
#need to ensure selection == 25 for this, as amended for a different analysis
serpas_linked_em <- final_EM4motif_results %>%
  pull(end_motif)
  
#making graphs for these serpas linked motifs
#adapting previously drawn lollipop graph
serpas_lollipop <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = final_EM4motif_results, aes(x = end_motif, y = cancer, colour = "Mean Cancer Values"), size = 3) +
 geom_point(data = final_EM4motif_results, aes(x = end_motif, y = control, colour = "Mean Control Values"), size = 3) +
  geom_segment(data = final_EM4motif_results, aes(x = end_motif, xend = end_motif, y = cancer, yend = control), colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  scale_colour_manual(
    name = "Treatment",
    values = c("Mean Cancer Values"= "magenta",
               "Mean Control Values" = "blue")
               
  ) +
  labs(y = "Relative Frequency",
       x = "End Motif",
       title = "25 Largest Control Values that Differ Significantly\n from the Cancer Value for that End-Motif") +
       #subtitle = "(Relative Frequency)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

serpas_lollipop
ggsave("serpas_lollipop.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)

```
```{r boxplots for serpas style data }



serpas_samples <- EM4[EM4$end_motif %in% serpas_linked_em, ]

ggplot(serpas_samples,
aes(x = end_motif,
y = frequency,
fill = treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.3) + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8)) +
  labs(y = "Relative Frequency",
       x = "End Motif",
       title = "End-Motifs selected to Serpas et al's Critera",
       subtitle = "Highest Control End-Motifs \nthat are significantly different in the Cancer State", 
       fill = "Treatment")

ggsave("serpas_samples.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)


```
```{r converting 4bp end-motifs to a meme file}

#choosing significant difference enriched for cacner
SigCancer_4bpEM <- final_EM4motif_results %>%
  filter(direction == "cancer-enriched") %>%
  pull(end_motif)%>%
  as.character()


#choosing significant difference enriched for control
SigControl_4bpEM <- final_EM4motif_results %>%
  filter(direction == "control-enriched") %>%
  pull(end_motif)%>%
  as.character()

#write a function to create a matrix for 4bp end-motifs
write_meme_file <- function(motifs, filename = "topsigDiff_4bp_EM.meme"){
  
bases <- c("A", "C", "G", "T")

con <- file(filename, open = "wt")
on.exit(close(con))

writeLines(
  c(
    "MEME version 4",
    "",
    "ALPHABET = ACGT",
    "",
    "strands: + -",
    "",
    "Background letter frequencies", 
    "A 0.25 C 0.25 G 0.25 T 0.25",
    ""
  ),
  con
)

for (motif in motifs) {
  motif_bases <- strsplit(motif, "")[[1]]
  
  motif_matrix <- t(
    sapply(motif_bases, function(base) {
      as.numeric(bases == base)
    })
  )
  
  writeLines(paste("MOTIF", motif), con)
  
  writeLines(
    "letter-probability matrix: alength= 4 w= 4 nsites= 1 E= 0",
    con
  )
  write.table(
    motif_matrix, 
    file = con,
    quote = FALSE,
    row.names = FALSE,
    col.names = FALSE
  )
  writeLines("", con)
}
  
}

#run the function for cancer enriched 4bp end motifs
write_meme_file(
  motifs = SigCancer_4bpEM,
  filename = "CancersigDiff_4bp_EM.meme"
)

#run the function for control enriched 4bp end motifs
write_meme_file(
  motifs = SigControl_4bpEM,
  filename = "ControlsigDiff_4bp_EM.meme"
)


```


```
```{r}


```

```{r }


  
  
  



  
  

```


```{r cleveland dot plot}
#not sure how useful this could be
ggplot(end_motif_means,
       aes(x = mean,
           y = reorder(end_motif, mean), colour = treatment)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(x = "mean value", y = "end_motif")

```
```{r making a graph on differences between cancer and control}

end_motif_mean_diff <- end_motif_means%>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = treatment, values_from = mean)%>%
  mutate(diff = cancer - control)

d <- ggplot(end_motif_mean_diff,
       aes(x = reorder(end_motif, diff),
           y = diff,
           fill = diff >0,
           text = paste(end_motif)))  +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "End Motif", y = "Difference (cancer - control)")

ggplotly(d, tooltip = "text")

ggsave("end_motif_mean_diff.png", plot = d)

interactive_em_mean_diff <- ggplotly(d)

htmlwidgets::saveWidget(interactive_em_mean_diff, "em_mean_diff.html", selfcontained = TRUE)

```


```{r the heatmap using ggplot}

pre_heatmap_df %>%
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = EM_source, y = end_motif)) +
  geom_tile(mapping = aes(fill = rel_freq)) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
  low = "blue",
  mid = "white",
  high = "red",
  midpoint = 0.01
  ) +
  theme(
  axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5)
) 


 
```
```{r converting df to matrix}



```






```{r end motif box plots}

end_motif_order <- EM4 %>%
filter(treatment == "cancer") %>%
group_by(end_motif) %>%
summarise(mean_freq = mean(frequency)) %>%
arrange(desc(frequency)) %>%
pull(end_motif)

EM4$end_motif <- factor(EM4$end_motif,
levels = end_motif_order )


b <- ggplot(EM4,
aes(x = end_motif,
y = frequency,
fill = treatment, text = end_motif)) +
geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(0.8))

ggplotly(b, tooltip = "text")

ggsave("EM_means.html", plot = b)

interactive_EM_means <- ggplotly(d)

htmlwidgets::saveWidget(interactive_EM_means, "EM_means.html", selfcontained = TRUE)

```


```
```{r }




``
